title: The Spirit of Might
reading:
There are things God calls you to that you are simply not capable of accomplishing by yourself. The Spirit of Might is the answer to that gap. Might is not personality. It is not talent or training, though God uses those. Might is supernatural strength released by the Spirit of God to accomplish what the natural cannot.

Samson's strength was not the result of his physique — he was set apart before birth, and the Spirit of the Lord came upon him at specific moments for specific purposes (Judges 14:6). Elijah calling down fire on Mount Carmel was not theater. It was a man fully consecrated to God making a direct confrontation with the false in full dependence on the real. When the fire fell, it consumed not just the sacrifice but the altar, the stones, the dust, and the water. That is Might — power so decisive it leaves no room for debate.

But Might is not only for dramatic moments. It is also for endurance. The same Spirit that enabled Elijah to outrun Ahab's chariot (1 Kings 18:46) later sustained him through the desert when he wanted to die. Paul writes in Ephesians 6:10 that believers are to "be strong in the Lord and in the power of His might" — not in the anticipation of a miracle, but in the daily demand of standing firm.

When Peter and John faced the lame man at the gate called Beautiful, they had no silver or gold. What they had was something the man had never encountered: "In the name of Jesus Christ of Nazareth, rise up and walk" (Acts 3:6). The Spirit of Might does not require that you have the resources. It requires that you have the authority.

You have been given both.
reflection:
Where in your life have you been waiting for natural ability before stepping into something God is asking of you?

What is the difference between confidence in your gifting and dependence on the Spirit of Might?

Is there a place where God is calling you to confront something — in prayer, in action, in spiritual warfare — and you have been holding back?
abide: Lord, I need more than determination. I need Your strength. Where I am facing something too large for my own capacity, let the Spirit of Might be my resource. Remind me that the battle belongs to You and that You are capable of accomplishing through me what I am powerless to accomplish on my own. Make me bold — not in myself, but in You.
